steps.title: Identify the explicit statement of why Miguel came
steps.content: The passage directly states his purpose: “Miguel was hoping not only to meet us but to make the acquaintance of the poet himself at Mamá's house.” This sentence reveals exactly why he accompanied his brother—a two-part objective.

steps.title: Match that intention to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D captures this precisely: Miguel “anticipates having the opportunity to be introduced to both the sisters and their father.” The other three add motives the passage never mentions: seeking mentorship (A), wanting instruction from the sisters (B), or planning to recite his work (C).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
